rulings.Case Title: WILLIAM FOLEY VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (BP) AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, October 7, 2008, line 2, DEFENDANT BP PRODUCTS NORTH AMERICA, INC.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT IS GRANTED. DEFENDANT SHIFTED THE BURDEN OF PRODUCING EVIDENCE BASED ON PLAINTIFF'S FACTUALLY DEVOID RESPONSES TO SPECIAL INTERROGATORIES AND PLAINTIFF'S TESTIMONY THAT HE DID NOT RECALL WORK BEING DONE THAT WOULD RELEASE ASBESTOS. (UMF 15 - 25.) PLAINTIFF FAILS TO PRESENT ADMISSIBLE EVIDENCE THAT CREATES A TRIABLE ISSUE OF FACT. AY'S DECLARATION IS NOT ADMISSIBLE TO PROVE PLAINTIFF WAS EXPOSED TO ASBESTOS ON DEFENDANT'S VESSELS. =(302/PJM/VC)

rulings.Case Title: KEARNEY STREET LAW OFFICES, INC., A CALIFORNIA VS. JOHN ARTHUR SEBASTINELLI et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, October 7, 2008, line 7, DEFENDANT JOHN SEBASTINELLI, PATRICIA SEBASTINELLI, MICHELMAN & ROBINSON, LLP MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION. Motion for summary adjudication on the fourth and fifth causes of action of Sebastinelli's cross complaint is denied. As to the fourth cause of action, Sebastinelli has shifted the burden of producing evidence by demonstrating that a loan was made and has not been repaid. There are triable issues of fact as to Kearney Street's right to offset its debt based on its trade secret claims. As to the fifth cause of action, Sebastinelli has failed to shift the burden of producing evidence by failing to establish the value of the securities at the time he tendered his shares. The Court also previously ruled that there is a triable issue of fact as to the validity of the shares tendered by Sebastinelli. =(302/PJM/PB)

rulings.Case Title: KEARNEY STREET LAW OFFICES, INC., A CALIFORNIA VS. JOHN ARTHUR SEBASTINELLI et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, October 7, 2008, line 7, DEFENDANT JOHN SEBASTINELLI, PATRICIA SEBASTINELLI, MICHELMAN & ROBINSON, LLP MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION. Motion for summary adjudication on the fifth, eigth, ninth and tenth causes of action to the third amended complaint is denied. Sebastenille fails to shift the burden of producing evidence. Sebastenelli has not demonstrated the absence of trade secrets but only that the secrets were not in a binder or kept under lock and key. The record reflects the existence of materials that are alleged to be trade secrets and steps taken to preserve confidentiality. The Aguillar Declaration reflects these facts as does Kearney Street's opposition to the moving parties' undisputed facts. =(302/PJM/PB)

rulings.Case Title: KEARNEY STREET LAW OFFICES, INC., A CALIFORNIA VS. JOHN ARTHUR SEBASTINELLI et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, October 7, 2008, line 7, DEFENDANT JOHN SEBASTINELLI, PATRICIA SEBASTINELLI, MICHELMAN & ROBINSON, LLP MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. Motion for summary adjudication on the first, second and fourth causes of action of the cross-complaint is denied. Sebastenilli has not established that he has paid any money to satisfy the obligation. If and when he does, this issue may be ripe for adjudication. At this point, there is no actual controversy. =(302/PJM/PB)

rulings.Case Title: GATEWAY BUSINESS BANK, A CALIFORNIA COLLECTION et al VS. VILLA DEI FIORI CONDOMINIUMS L.P. A CALIFORNIA et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendants And Cross-Complainants Villa Dei Fiori Condominiums, L.P., Innovative Realty Services, Llc, Brian S. Baniqued, Brian S. Baniqued Trustee Of The Baniqued 2003 Family Trust And Tehotis P. Oliphant'S Order To Show Cause Regarding Preliminary Injunction
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, October 7, 2008, line 9, Defendants And Cross-Complainants Villa Dei Fiori Condominiums, L.P., Innovative Realty Services, LLC, Brian S. Baniqued, Brian S. Baniqued Trustee Of The Baniqued 2003 Family Trust And Tehotis P. Oliphant's Order To Show Cause Regarding Preliminary Injunction. Hearing required. The promissory note does not preclude an oral modification and as a consequence, this case differs from Faucchi & Limi Farms v. United Agri Products (9th Cir. 2004) 414 F.3d 1075. The parties had done so in July and October 2007 and evidence has been presented of a further oral modification on January 6, 2008. The parties are to address the consideration for the oral modification. Novation requires clear and convincing evidence proof of a new contract. Again, the question is what is the consideration for the new contract. Plaintiff contends that many of the communications that occurred after January 6, 2008 are not admissible because they are part of settlement discussions not evidence of the absence of an agreement. This issue must be addressed. Plaintiff has established irreparable harm if the sale goes through. If the motion were to be granted, what bond should be required? =(302/PJM/JW)
